lesson.title: Lesson 10: The Loss of the Titanic

rights.notes: Migrated from legacy published content; migration does not establish redistribution rights.

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: What would have happened if only two of the sixteen watertight compartments had been flooded?

translation.text: 如果16个水密舱中只有两个进水，会发生什么？

segments.text: The great ship Titanic sailed for New York from Southampton on April 10, 1912.

translation.text: 巨轮“泰坦尼克号”于1912年4月10日从南安普敦启航驶往纽约。

segments.text: She was carrying 1,316 passengers and a crew of 891.

translation.text: 船上载有1,316名乘客和891名船员。

segments.text: Even by modern standards, the 46,000-ton Titanic was a colossal ship.

translation.text: 即使用现代标准衡量，这艘46,000吨的“泰坦尼克号”也是一艘庞然大物。

segments.text: At that time, however, she was not only the largest ship that had ever been built, but was regarded as unsinkable, for she had sixteen watertight compartments.

translation.text: 然而在当时，它不仅是有史以来建造的最大船只，而且因拥有16个水密舱而被认为永不沉没。

segments.text: Even if two of these were flooded, she would still be able to float.

translation.text: 即使其中两个进水，它仍能漂浮。

segments.text: The tragic sinking of this great liner will always be remembered, for she went down on her first voyage with heavy loss of life.

translation.text: 这艘巨型客轮的悲惨沉没将永远为人铭记，因为它在处女航中沉没，造成了惨重的人员伤亡。

segments.text: Four days after setting out, while the Titanic was sailing across the icy waters of the North Atlantic, a huge iceberg was suddenly spotted by a lookout.

translation.text: 启航四天后，当“泰坦尼克号”航行在北大西洋冰冷的海面上时，瞭望员突然发现了一座巨大的冰山。

segments.text: After the alarm had been given, the great ship turned sharply to avoid a direct collision.

translation.text: 警报发出后，巨轮急转弯以避免正面相撞。

segments.text: The Titanic turned just in time, narrowly missing the immense wall of ice which rose over one hundred feet out of the water beside her.

translation.text: “泰坦尼克号”转向及时，与船旁高出水面100多英尺的巨大冰墙擦身而过。

segments.text: Suddenly, there was a slight, trembling sound from below, and the captain went down to see what had happened.

translation.text: 突然，船下传来轻微的震颤声，船长下去查看出了什么事。

segments.text: The noise had been so faint that no one thought that the ship had been damaged.

translation.text: 声音非常轻，以至于没有人认为船体已经受损。

segments.text: the captain realized, to his horror, that the Titanic was sinking rapidly, for five of her sixteen watertight compartments had already been flooded.

translation.text: 船长惊恐地意识到“泰坦尼克号”正在迅速下沉，因为16个水密舱中已有5个进水。

segments.text: The order to abandon ship was given, and hundreds of people plunged into the icy water.

translation.text: 弃船命令发出后，数百人跳入冰冷的海水。

segments.text: As there were not enough lifeboats for everybody, one thousand five hundred lives were lost.

translation.text: 由于救生艇不够所有人使用，共有1,500人丧生。
